title: RGL-80 electronet grenade

The '''RGL-80 electronet grenade''', also known as an '''electronet grenade''' was a type of [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] designed by [[Golan Arms/Legends|Golan Arms]] to [[Stun/Legends|stun]] and capture targets.

==Description==
The RGL-80 electronet grenade was manufactured by [[Golan Arms/Legends|Golan Arms]]. An [[Stun net/Legends|electronet]] was loaded into the [[Warhead/Legends|warhead]] of a [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]]-style canister round and fired from any standard [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launcher]],<ref name="GG10" /> or a speciality [[Net gun/Legends|net launcher]] like Golan's [[RGL-80A electronet grenade launcher]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> Once fired, the grenade trailed a control wire attached to a control panel affixed to the underside of the launcher. Once triggered by the operator, the warhead would detonate, discharging the outer casing and allowing the net to expand to its full, two-[[Meter/Legends|meter]] diameter.<ref name="GG10" />

Once the [[Net/Legends|net]] enveloped a target—commonly a living being—they would be ensnared. The operator of the launcher could then send a variable electrical charge through the net, shocking the trapped target. As the charge that could be put through the net was adjustable, the target could be [[Stun/Legends|stunned]], or even [[Death/Legends|killed]] in extreme circumstances.

A single electronet grenade weighed five [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]]<ref name="SAV" /> and a magazine of five of them cost 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. The RGL-80 electronet grenade had a maximum deployable range of five hundred meters.<ref name="GG10" /> 

==History==
The electronet grenade was developed by the company [[Golan Arms/Legends|Golan Arms]]. In the days that followed the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], it was hard to obtain, but it did find some use between [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] who wanted to capture fugitive prey. One bounty hunter who employed this weapon was [[Garim Ayrvn]].<ref name="GG10" /> Another was [[Remel Fud]], who relied heavily on his technology to bring down his targets.<ref name="Not-So Magnificent Seven">{{SW|url=news/the-not-so-magnificent-seven|text=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|int=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|nolive=1}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The RGL-80 electronet grenade was first mentioned in [[1994]]'s ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', written for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.
